name: Automated Web Scraping and AI Price Analysis — HTTP + HTML + GPT-4o-mini + Sheets + Gmail

parameters.content:
## Automated Web Scraping and AI Price Analysis — HTTP + HTML + GPT-4o-mini + Google Sheets + Gmail

For competitor monitoring, price tracking, and product research — a daily automated scraper that turns any product listing page into a structured dataset with an AI market briefing. Every day at 9 AM the workflow fetches a target website, extracts every product's title and price, sorts them by price, saves them to Google Sheets, and builds a CSV. GPT-4o-mini reads the price statistics and writes a short plain-text market insight. An email is sent with the AI insight in the body and the full product CSV attached. Works on any website — change the URL and the CSS selectors to match your target.

## How it works
- **1. Schedule Trigger — Daily 9AM** fires once per day at 09:00
- **2. HTTP — Fetch Website HTML** downloads the full page HTML
- **3. HTML — Extract All Products** pulls every product container as an HTML array using a CSS selector
- **4. Split Out — One Product at a Time** turns the array into individual items
- **5. HTML — Extract Title and Price** extracts the title attribute and price text from each product
- **6. Sort — By Price Descending** orders products from highest to lowest price
- **7. Code — Add Date and Clean Price** adds the scrape date and a numeric price field
- **8. Google Sheets — Save Products** appends every product row (parallel branch)
- **9. Code — Compile for AI and Stats** computes min, max, average price and builds the AI context (parallel branch)
- **10. AI Agent — Price Analysis** uses GPT-4o-mini to write a short market insight
- **OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** language model attached to the AI Agent
- **11. Convert to CSV File** converts all product rows into a downloadable CSV (parallel branch)
- **12. Merge — Combine AI and CSV** waits for both the AI insight and the CSV before continuing, so the email is sent exactly once
- **13. Code — Merge CSV and AI for Email** combines the CSV binary with the AI insight and stats
- **14. Gmail — Send Report and CSV** emails the HTML report with stats, the AI insight, and the CSV attached

## Set up steps
1. By default the workflow scrapes books.toscrape.com (a free practice site). To scrape a different site, change the URL in node 2 and update the CSS selectors in nodes 3 and 5 using your browser DevTools (F12 → Inspect)
2. In **OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI API credential
3. In **8. Google Sheets — Save Products** — connect Google Sheets OAuth2 and replace `YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID`. Create a tab named Products with columns: Title, Price, Scraped Date
4. In **14. Gmail — Send Report and CSV** — connect Gmail OAuth2 and replace `YOUR_EMAIL_ADDRESS`

nodes.name: Section — Daily Fetch and Product Extraction

parameters.content:
## Daily Fetch and Product Extraction
Schedule fires daily at 9 AM. HTTP downloads the full page HTML. HTML node extracts every product container as an array using a CSS selector.

nodes.name: Section — Split, Extract Fields, and Sort

parameters.content:
## Split, Extract Fields, and Sort
Split Out turns the product array into individual items. HTML node extracts title and price per item. Sort orders products by price descending.

nodes.name: Section — Date Cleanup, Sheets Save, Stats, and AI Analysis

parameters.content:
## Date Cleanup, Sheets Save, Stats, and AI Analysis
Code adds the scrape date and numeric price. Three parallel branches: Sheets saves all rows, Code compiles price stats which GPT-4o-mini analyzes into a market insight, and CSV conversion runs alongside.

## CSV Merge and Email
Merge waits for both the AI insight and the CSV file so the email fires exactly once. Code combines the CSV binary with the AI insight and stats. Gmail sends the report with the CSV attached.

nodes.name: 4. Split Out — One Product at a Time
7. Code — Add Date and Clean Price
9. Code — Compile for AI and Stats

options.systemMessage:
You are a market research analyst. You have been given product scraping data from an online store. Write a short, useful business intelligence summary.

Your summary must include:
1. One sentence on overall price range and what it tells about the market segment
2. One observation about the most expensive products — what category or pattern do they share?
3. One observation about the cheapest products
4. One actionable insight for a buyer, seller, or researcher using this data

Keep it under 150 words. Write in plain text — no markdown, no bullet points, no headers. Write like a smart analyst giving a quick verbal briefing.
